Alright, you're back. Let's move forward. Now here's the deal. In order to successfully peel back these layers of straight line prospecting for you, there's a powerful visual layer I want to share with you that lays out the basic pathway that each prospect takes starting from that first moment they enter your pipeline. Now the industry term for this is called a sales funnel and understanding it is a crucial aspect of mastering the straight line system. So with that in mind, let me start with a basic overview of what a sales funnel actually looks like and then once you understand that and have the appropriate context, I'll then take you through some specific examples for different types of sales funnels based on what the product you're selling and how it's being sold, meaning is it in person or over Now notice here that I left out the internet on purpose. There's a reason for that. Let me explain. I struggle with this a bit when I was creating this lesson for you because nowadays, you know, everyone talks about my funnel. I'll build your funnel. I'll make your funnel. It can drive you crazy. But that's a different type of funnel. That is a marketing funnel, meaning you're driving traffic to a specific website where there is essentially a landing page. There's a headline, a video, a call to action, upsells, downsells, cross-sells, the whole nine yards. That's not a sales funnel. That's a marketing funnel. And while it's crucial to success for an online marketer and for a businessman learning how to drive traffic and get leads, for the purposes of sales, it's an entirely different thing. So I decided not to include it because frankly, it's a four to five hour chunk of content and it really falls into the marketing category, not sales. Make sense? Okay, great. So take a moment here to click the button that best represents your current sales process. It's strictly phone-based, meaning you start off by making an outgoing telemarketing call. Then you set an appointment, which is going to be followed up by phone. In other words, everything is over the phone, nothing out in the field. That's number one. Number two, you start off by making a call, but you then set an appointment to meet this prospect out in the field, either at their business or their home. That's your second option, hybrid. Number three, are you a work instead of store, meaning retail or even a car dealership, where people essentially are coming in your storefront? That's your first point of contact. That's number three. Number four, are you out there in the field knocking on doors toward a salesperson? Right? And lastly, number five, are you getting your leads from the internet, from some of the online marketing funnel, at which point you then follow those up by telephone? Those are your five choices, okay? Choose the one that best describes your current sales process, and I'll take you through the corresponding funnel right now. All right, perfect. You chose that you're strictly a phone-based salesperson. Guess what? If you also chose that you get your leads on the internet and then follow up by phone while you're in the same group, and let me tell you why. See, whether you're just doing outright telemarketing, meaning you're dying for dollars, making hundreds of calls a day, or if you're getting your leads from some type of online resource, could be a marketing funnel, even an aggregator, where you're actually buying leads from people who just aggregate leads for a specific industry, right? Either way, when those leads come into your company, to your funnel, so to speak, right? Your first point of contact is going to be over the phone. So the only difference between strictly phone-based sales funnels and an internet funnel that goes into the telephone is that with the internet funnel, you're probably going to have a limited number of leads, unless you're getting a massive quantity of them. Those, when you're just telemarketing, outgoing, you typically have just an unlimited lead source, so that top number at the wide end of your funnel, I'll explain that in a moment, right, is going to be much larger, but with the internet-based deal, it's going to be small. You get it? In other words, there'll be a lot less leads to call through, typically, if you're getting them from an online source versus telemarketing from a list. Either way, I'll explain it as we go along here, but you're together, YouTube groups, right? So let's cut to a graphic right now, and I'll show you how this plays out as a sales funnel. Now, it always starts out here, what we call the wide end of the funnel, the number of dials that you make. So for example, if you're getting your own leads from the internet, well, how many leads are coming into your pipeline? That's your wide end, you know, how many leads do you have to call? Conversely, if you're dialing the dials from a list, you'll probably have a lot more, so you can just keep dialing and dialing all day. So let's use a number here of 100 dials. We're going to use that as a number of outgoing calls you make that represents the wide end of the funnel. Now, one thing to caution here about is that when you're out there really dialing for dials on a phone, you could actually make a heck of a lot more calls than 100 in one day. So I'm just using that number of 100 because it's a round number. You could actually make 200, even 300 calls a day. But again, we'll start for the 100. And if you're getting less leads than that from the internet, then what you'll do is as we move down the funnel, you just have to adjust your ratios to reflect that. You'll see in a moment. Okay, so watch now as we move down this funnel here, what I want you to notice is that if you made 100 calls, well, guess what? You're not going to get in touch with 100 human beings. You've got in touch with maybe 50 human beings. That is, only 50 will answer the phone. The other 50, well, that could be wrong numbers, phone could have rang off the hook, or perhaps you've got that most dreaded of all things, the answering machine. We all hate it. Now there are strategies for whether or not you leave a message, but let's not deal with that right now. It's a separate issue. We'll cover it in a separate part of the trading for right now. I just want you to focus on how you actually move down the funnel. So take it from me here. This is a fact. If you dial a phone 100 times, you'll only get it in touch with approximately 50 people. Get it? Then going down the funnel further from there, notice how it gets narrow because you'll only get in touch with perhaps 25 people who are the actual decision makers. Meaning this is the point in the funnel where you're actually going to get screened out by gatekeepers, perhaps someone's secretary, their assistant. In other words, you call someone and you can't get through to the decision maker. It could be at home by the way, where a kid answers the phone, a maid, whatever happens. In other words, you called, you spoke to a live human being, but it wasn't the actual decision maker. So meaning that these are the ones who you're going to want to actually make a presentation to. You're not going to present to a gatekeeper or to a 10-year-old kid or to a maid. You get it? It's a waste of time and I'll cover that later on in this training actually in this module. But the point here is that 50 people speak to you, only 25 of them are actual decision makers. And perhaps from there going down even further, only 10 of them will become qualified leads. Meaning that when you start asking them questions to gather intelligence, you find out that they actually need your product, they want your product, they can afford your product, and now you've set up an appointment for them to speech you again in person. In other words, remember we're on our strictly phone-based funnel here. So what you're doing here is that you're setting up for the next call, which is a follow-up presentation. Now again, notice that as you move down the line here, down the funnel, the number of appointments keeps getting smaller. Now you have 10 appointments, but that won't turn into 10 presentations. Next number is what? Five. In other words, when you set an appointment, that doesn't mean you can actually speak to the person again. I know it kind of stinks here, but the reality is, is that when you're on the phone and then you follow up with someone who's set in the point with you, half of the time you're not going to speak to these people ever again, which is one of the reasons why when we get to sales cycles, I'm going to be showing you strategies of how to collapse those two calls into one. You get it? But either away here, the point is that this is where that number of 10 qualify leads is going to be diminished somewhat. We'll use five as an example here. These are the people who actually get on the phone with you a second time, and that's where they came in from the internet or they came in from telemarketing. We're at that point now. We're actually having that second real closing presentation. So the first call was a qualifying call. Second call is now a closing call, and both are being done over the telephone. Now again, there are strategies that I'm going to teach you that can help you increase this ratio substantially, meaning the people who actually get on the phone a second time. But here I just want to show you the funnel of how it works first conceptually. So now of these five people, let's say your closing rate is 40%. That means on average, you're going to close two of these people that will actually buy from you. And let's say your commission on that is $500 per sale. That's your average, right? That means you would make on that particular day $1,000. All right, so you're out there in the field knocking on doors. My true love. Again, this was the first job I had. It broke all the records. I know this so well. Something really latest out to you in detail. I think it's going to be really helpful. So now in this case, the wide end of your funnel will be represented by what? How many doors you knock on each day. Now, just so you know, that number can be as high as 200 even 250 a day. You can really ratchet up that number. For example, you got to train yourself to go into these concentrated areas where there's lots of doors to knock on as opposed to being in an area where there's only one door every mile or so. Right? It's going to be very difficult to generate your numbers when the doors are far away from each other. So just be aware of that. But again, you got to match that with finding your best chance of getting qualified buyers. Okay. Anyway, the point here is that the wide end of your funnel, it simply represents how many times your knuckles bang on a door. So let's just choose 100 because it's a round number. But now notice how this quickly drops off now to 50 because sometimes no one's home. Now it'll be probably around half the time when you're knocking on doors out in the field, right? And then notice again how that number drops again to 25. Well, what that drop represents is that often the door will open but the person who answers is not a decision maker and you can't get that person to go get the appropriate person or they're simply not home. In other words, it could be amazing answers to the door or child or anybody else. Point being is that you've either gotten screened out, they won't let the decision maker come to the door or they're simply not home. But remember, you don't want to be pitching 10-year-old kids or pitching maids, you get it, you want to only make presentations to decision makers. Now I want to really slow things down here at this point as we can even move down the funnel. So notice that if we have 25 decision makers, you're probably only going to get 10 qualified leads from that. And then the temple said, yeah, yeah, I'm interested and then send information or leave information and come back for another appointment, which could be another appointment in person or it could be over the phone. But either way, what happens though is if you have 10 appointments set, notice again, the number drops down to five for actual follow up presentations. Very often, for instance, a lot of people out there knocking on doors, they call them openers. So they go out there, they might get 10 qualified leads per day, but of those 10 leads, only five of them end up turning into in-person appointments to close. Why? People change their mind, people are hard to get in touch with twice. You have that attrition rate, just part of the business. You simply can't get in touch with them twice. And guess what? It is annoying, very annoying, which is why we often try to collapse this down to a one-call close. You get it. In other words, when you're in person with the power of body language and face-to-face sales, very often we can do that. We can actually close them in one call. Just be aware that if you're using a two-call system, you're going to lose perhaps half of those people. So you'll go from 10 people who say, yeah, I'm interested, you know, leave me some info and set up another appointment or send me some, email me some, right? But five of them will only be back there for the next appointment. Kind of stinks, you know? So we try then to collapse that to one call because let's say your closing rate is 40%. Well, now that means you're only going to end up closing here two people in a day because the 10 turned to five. The other bars you never got to speak to. You're going to make so a quarter million dollars a year. Pretty powerful, right? Just remember though, if you could somehow collapse that to one call, well, guess what? Of course, there are things that we can do to reduce that bounce rate. In other words, it typically amounts to someone walking in and they thought they were going to find something else, like the advertising and the look and feel of the store not congruent. That's a very common reason why people bounce, both in stores and in online. Like if their landing page is not congruent with the advertiser, they get paid online, people say, oh, that's not what I thought they bounce out. Now, the next number here that really matters is you're going to go from 50 down to 25. Why? And remember, as a straight line salesperson, we don't pitch, we don't present to unqualified people because it wastes our time and gives us banned read. It's about skills, lead generation closing, but on the wide end, wow, it's like just so simple. You want to make a certain amount of money. Once you know what your ratios are, it's actually quite simple. You back into it. You look backwards. You say, okay, here I am. I'm making currently 150 grand. I want to make 300 grand. Guess what? Double the amount of dials you make. Knock on twice as many doors. Increase the amount of leads that you're actually purchasing, or increase your marketing or focus on that. Whatever is the easiest way to use this funnel is to say, you know what? How many of the top line things I can always control? How many doors should I knock on? How many times should I dial and so forth? Once you do that, you can really use this with great effectiveness to back into a certain amount of money that you want to make. Another thing, once you actually have this funnel developed and you have enough information that the numbers are statistically significant, well, then you actually start tracking it and tracking it. And if all of a sudden you get into a slump, you can say, hey, wait, what's changed? Am I not getting through enough gatekeepers? Maybe I need to work on that. Maybe I'm not sounding. My tonalities are off. Or you say, maybe my closing radius dropped. Hmm, I got to work on my closes. You can use this also as a diagnostic tool. The key here is to make sure that you keep track of your numbers. If you have an automated CRM like Salesforce.com, fabulous. If you don't, then do it by hand, but make sure that you keep track of things. All right. And with that, let's go to a really important quiz right now. And also some exercises.
